column1: Impact Assessment hands drying: Hands-on Learning impact assessment on your own
1. Use the inventory data and characterization factorsfrom part A to fill in the impact assessment tables below (e.g. cells B41 to D44) to estimate for the two hand dryer scenarios
the respiratory inorganics (fine particulate) and global warming impacts on human health, first at midpoint level
(in kgPM2.5 equivalent/FU for PM and kg CO2 equivalent/FU for Global warming )
2. Based on these results, identify  the substance dominating  substances and discuss this at the bottom of each table (e.g. cell B49)
3. Use the Midpoint to Damage factors to fill-in the bottom of each impact assessment table (e.g cells D45 to D48)  and to calculate damages in DALY/FU
4. Based on the midpoint and damage results, fill in the summary table (C66 to D68) linking it to the final results of each table and discuss what is the best of the two scenarios
from a human health perspective (considering these two impact categories) and what is the dominant impact category for each scenario (cells C70 & C71)
